location.origin: Born circa 1996, Renton/Kent area, Washington State. Parents are Indonesian immigrants who led a worship group at their church and operate the Bumbu Truck (Indonesian street food -- satay and lumpia). Family spent time in Maryland during her youth, where she performed annually on Indonesian Independence Day in youth choirs and orchestras, learning traditional Indonesian folk songs. Father 'taught me how to listen' and mother 'taught me how to sing.' Family valued music as a hobby, not a career -- traditional Indonesian career expectations (medicine, law, engineering) were the presumed destination. Started piano lessons as a child (disliked them). Played trombone in elementary school band. Self-taught on guitar, ukulele, harmonica, and drums. Discovered FruityLoops (FL Studio) at age 14 and began self-teaching electronic production.
location.formative: Seattle/Cornish College of the Arts (late 2010s): Studied music composition at Cornish, initially focused on scoring films. Studied gamelan ensemble at Cornish -- described it as 'one of the most beautiful complex genres ever.' Intentionally incorporates gamelan influences into electronic productions. Self-taught on FL Studio for seven years before sound clicked. Breakthrough track 'Pompelo' (May 2018) marked the point where imitation ended and original creation began. Worked as a line cook at Kizuki Ramen on Capitol Hill while building music career. Entered beat-making competition in 2017 -- lost in second round but met future manager. First shows in 2018. The Cornish connection bridges degree 44 directly to degree 43 (Ann Wilson attended Cornish in the late 1960s).
location.career: Seattle, Washington (career base, 2016-present; recently relocated to Los Angeles). Debut EP Love Memo (Valentine's Day 2018, self-released) -- earned 'Top Album' from The Seattle Times, Seattle Weekly, The Stranger, KEXP, 107.7 The End. Split vinyl with Stas THEE Boss (S'Women) on Crane City Music (750 numbered copies, October 2018). Singles: 'Pompelo' (May 2018), 'Waves,' 'Two Colors' (April 2019, KEXP premiere), 'Take Two' feat. Hollis Wong-Wear (2020, KEXP Song of the Day, produced with Roget Chahayed). Co-produced 'Lazy Susan' on Marvel's Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings soundtrack (88rising/Interscope, August 2021) featuring 21 Savage, Rich Brian, Warren Hue, Masiwei -- produced the beat switch section. 'Wrote A Lil Song' with Maiah Manser (Hardly Art/Sub Pop, July 2022). Co-produced Wafia's Atlantic single 'Pick Me' (2020). Debut album Do We Make of This? (June 14, 2024, self-released, 11 tracks, 33 min). Sync placements: Singapore Airlines, Apple, Universal Audio, Nike, Maybelline, Amazon Modern Love, Freeform The Bold Type, Comedy Central Nora from Queens, Lucifer. Shared stages with ODESZA, Death Cab for Cutie, The 1975, Japanese Breakfast, Toro Y Moi, Head and the Heart, Omar Apollo, K.Flay. Featured in Gary Campbell's NEWCOMER: A Seattle Hip-Hop Mixtape documentary (2020). Visit Seattle 'I Know A Place' video series host. Sonic Guild grant recipient (2020). Signed to TruSauce production label (mentor: Roget Chahayed, 2019-present). Walked red carpet at Shang-Chi premiere (2021). Anthony Fantano (The Needle Drop) praised her work. WASH Magazine called Do We Make of This? 'one of the greatest local records of the year.' Stage name from Avatar: The Last Airbender character Chong -- chosen partly for gender-neutral quality. Identifies as queer woman of color (documented in KEXP conversation with Stas THEE Boss). Multi-instrumentalist: piano, guitar, ukulele, harmonica, trombone, drums. Primary production tools: FL Studio, Roland SP-404A sampler, Roland keyboard, iPhone voice memos. Primary sonic elements: sawtooth synthesizers, 808 bass, chopped vocal samples, field recordings. Key influences: Sophie Xeon ('my North star'), Daft Punk, Pharrell Williams, J Dilla, Benny Blanco, Solange Knowles, Javanese gamelan.

labels: Self-released (Love Memo EP 2018, Do We Make of This? LP 2024, all primary singles)
Crane City Music (split vinyl with Stas THEE Boss, 750 copies, October 2018)
Hardly Art / Sub Pop (Wrote A Lil Song with Maiah Manser, July 2022)
88rising / Interscope (Lazy Susan co-production on Shang-Chi soundtrack, 2021)
TruSauce (production label, mentor Roget Chahayed, signed 2020)

members.name: Chong the Nomad (Alda Agustiano)
members.note: Born circa 1996, Renton/Kent, Washington. Indonesian-American. Parents are Indonesian immigrants. Studied music composition and gamelan at Cornish College of the Arts. Self-taught on FL Studio since age 14. Multi-instrumentalist: piano, guitar, ukulele, harmonica, trombone, drums. Stage name from Avatar: The Last Airbender character Chong -- chosen partly for gender neutrality. Identifies as queer woman of color. Worked as line cook at Kizuki Ramen on Capitol Hill. Family operates Bumbu Truck (Indonesian street food). Sonic Guild grant recipient 2020. Featured in NEWCOMER documentary (2020). Visit Seattle I Know A Place host. Marvel Shang-Chi red carpet (2021). Recently relocated to Los Angeles.

associated_musicians.name: Roget Chahayed
associated_musicians.note: Grammy-nominated producer. Agustiano signed to his TruSauce production label in 2020. Working together since 2019. Co-produced 'Lazy Susan' (Shang-Chi), 'Take Two' (with Hollis), additional production on 'Before It Checks Out' (Do We Make of This? track 11). Key career mentor who connected Agustiano to major-label production world.

associated_musicians.name: Stas THEE Boss (Stasia Irons)
associated_musicians.role: Scene collaborator, split LP partner, KEXP Street Sounds host
associated_musicians.note: Seattle DJ, producer, former member of THEESatisfaction. Split vinyl pairing S'Women with Love Memo on Crane City Music (2018). KEXP in-conversation feature (2018). Both queer women of color producers -- a rare combination in the scene. 'Undervelvet' collaboration. First met at Capitol Hill Block Party.

associated_musicians.name: Hollis Wong-Wear
associated_musicians.note: Grammy-nominated Seattle artist, member of Macklemore's team. Soulful vocal collaboration produced in Los Angeles with Roget Chahayed. KEXP Song of the Day.

associated_musicians.name: Maiah Manser
associated_musicians.role: Vocalist, collaborator on 'Wrote A Lil Song' (2022)
associated_musicians.note: Los Angeles-based vocalist, producer, and visual artist. Collaboration released on Hardly Art (Sub Pop subsidiary). Ethereal electronic textures.

associated_musicians.name: 21 Savage
associated_musicians.note: Agustiano produced the beat switch section where 21 Savage enters. Added 'robot noises' and electronic textures.

associated_musicians.name: Rich Brian
associated_musicians.note: 88rising artist. Indonesian-born rapper. The Indonesian connection between Agustiano and Rich Brian on a Marvel soundtrack is culturally significant.

associated_musicians.name: Warren Hue
associated_musicians.note: 88rising artist. Indonesian rapper. Further extends the Indonesian diaspora connection.

associated_musicians.name: Taylor Dexter (Tay Dex)
associated_musicians.note: L.A. producer. Guitar sample on '4 Mistakes' (Do We Make of This? track 3). Co-producer on 'Lazy Susan.'

associated_musicians.name: Wesley Singerman
associated_musicians.note: Additional production on 'Lend Me Your Ears' (Do We Make of This? track 10). Co-producer on 'Lazy Susan.'

associated_musicians.name: Gary Campbell
associated_musicians.role: Crane City Music founder, NEWCOMER documentary filmmaker
associated_musicians.note: Seattle filmmaker and independent hip-hop vinyl label operator. Proposed the Chong/Stas split LP. Documented Agustiano's rise from Belltown Yacht Club to CID Block Party in NEWCOMER: A Seattle Hip-Hop Mixtape (2020).

associated_musicians.name: Wafia
associated_musicians.note: Iraqi-Australian singer signed to Atlantic Records. Co-production credit extends Agustiano into major-label R&B/pop.

theory_nodes.concept: The grain (forty-fifth acoustic element -- the sonic phenomenon produced when a sound source is decomposed into microscopic grains, individually processed, and reassembled into a texture that shimmers between recognition and abstraction; the first digital-production-physics acoustic element in the engine)
examples: Chong the Nomad (degree 44) -- chopped vocal samples manipulated for pitch and texture until unrecognizable produce the grain. The wordless vocal harmonies opening 'Find Me Here' shimmer through granular processing. The 'stuttering fragments of noise' in album breakdowns are granular events: the beat decomposed into grains, each individually processed, the whole reassembled into a shimmering texture.

Contrast with the radiance (degree 43, Ann Wilson) -- analog vocal projection producing coherent overtones. The grain is digital audio manipulation producing particle-based textures. Both are acoustic phenomena; one from a body in a room, the other from a mind operating a computer.

Connection to gamelan kotekan -- interlocking metallophone patterns that produce a composite shimmer from individual incomplete parts. Agustiano's electronic granular techniques achieve the same shimmering composite effect through digital means. Gamelan shimmer and grain are structural analogues across acoustic and digital domains.
The progression from radiance to grain is the progression from analog to digital, from the voice that fills a room to the laptop that fills headphones.
theory_nodes.description: The grain is the forty-fifth acoustic element in the Seattle 360 engine. It describes the sonic phenomenon produced when sound is decomposed into microscopic grains (typically 1-100 milliseconds), individually processed, and reassembled into a texture that shimmers between recognition and abstraction. This is the acoustic signature of sophisticated electronic production: a vocal sample, field recording, or synthesizer tone broken into particles so small the source becomes unrecognizable, yet the emotional quality persists as luminous, shimmering texture. Chong the Nomad's chopped vocal samples, processed synthesizer layers, and fragmented beats all produce the grain. It is the first acoustic element describing digital production physics, complementing the radiance (degree 43) which described analog vocal projection physics.

ancestors: The radiance (degree 43, Ann Wilson) -- analog vocal projection. The grain is digital audio manipulation. Consecutive elements, analog-to-digital progression.
The sonic boom (degree 36, Jimi Hendrix) -- instrument-generated acoustic event. The grain is DAW-generated. Different eras of instrument transformation.
The whisper (degree 31, Brenda Lee Eager) -- minimal vocal energy. The grain can reduce any sound to whisper-level grains.
genealogy.relationship: Forty-five acoustic elements across forty-five degrees. The grain is the engine's first digital-production element. It marks the transition from documenting analog acoustic phenomena to documenting digital production phenomena. Track element 46 at degree 45.

theory_nodes.concept: The bedroom architect (forty-fifth artist-city pattern -- an artist who builds an entire sonic world from a bedroom or home studio within the city, without institutional scaffolding of labels, studios, or gatekeepers; the city provides cultural context but creative work happens in private; first technology-mediated city pattern)
examples: Chong the Nomad (degree 44) -- productions made with FruityLoops in a personal space. iPhone voice memos captured ideas in transit. SP-404A sampler in a home setup. The city entered the work through cultural osmosis: Capitol Hill venues, KEXP curation, Crane City Music community, 206 Zulu, CID Block Party. All absorbed and transformed in the private studio.

Contrast with the scene native (degree 41, Shawn Smith) -- embedded in public music spaces. The scene native plays the clubs, sits in on sessions, is physically present. The bedroom architect creates in isolation and connects through performance and streaming.
Contrast with the lifer (degree 43, Ann Wilson) -- city connection spanning a lifetime of public activity. The bedroom architect's connection is private, internal, technology-mediated.

The pattern reflects the structural transformation of twenty-first-century music-making: from shared physical spaces (clubs, studios, churches) to private production spaces connected to scenes through technology.
theory_nodes.description: The bedroom architect is the forty-fifth artist-city pattern in the Seattle 360 engine. It describes an artist who builds an entire sonic world from a bedroom, home studio, or personal production space, without the institutional scaffolding of labels, studios, or industry gatekeepers. The city provides cultural context (Seattle's electronic scene, KEXP, Capitol Hill, Crane City Music), but the creative work happens in private with a laptop and headphones. The city is the soil; the bedroom is the greenhouse. Chong the Nomad exemplifies this: FruityLoops productions in a private space, iPhone voice memos in transit, SP-404A sampler at home, Capitol Hill culture absorbed through osmosis and transformed in isolation. The bedroom architect is the first technology-mediated city pattern -- prior patterns assumed music-making in shared physical spaces.

ancestors: The lifer (degree 43, Ann Wilson) -- public lifetime connection. The bedroom architect is private technology-mediated connection.
The scene native (degree 41, Shawn Smith) -- embedded in public spaces. The bedroom architect works in private.
The cultural satellite (degree 40, Alice Smith) -- remote connection. The bedroom architect is local but private.
genealogy.relationship: Forty-five artist-city patterns across forty-five degrees. The bedroom architect is the engine's first pattern reflecting how technology has changed the relationship between artist and city. Prior patterns assumed co-location in shared spaces. Track pattern 46 at degree 45.

theory_nodes.concept: Blue note -- the chopped bend (forty-fourth deployment; the blue note captured in a vocal sample, sliced into fragments, pitch-shifted, and embedded in an electronic beat so that the emotional content of the blues bend persists through digital processing; first digitally mediated blue note)
examples: Chong the Nomad (degree 44) -- vocal fragments in Love Memo and Do We Make of This? carry the emotional inflection of R&B vocal bending (rise and fall signaling longing, loss, tenderness) but delivered through digital processing that abstracts the bend into pure texture. The listener feels the blue note without hearing a traditional vocal performance.

Contrast with the power bend (degree 43, Ann Wilson) -- blue note at maximum analog volume. The chopped bend is blue note at maximum digital density. Consecutive deployments, opposite media: one fills a room, the other fills a frequency spectrum.
Contrast with the sanctified bend (degree 42, Erica Campbell) -- spiritual upward vector. The chopped bend is fragmented into particles -- neither upward nor outward but dispersed.

The chopped bend proves the blues tradition is not dependent on analog delivery. The voice sings the bend; the DAW captures it; the producer slices it; the beat carries it. The blue note's emotional DNA survives four stages of digital processing.
theory_nodes.description: The chopped bend is the forty-fourth blue note deployment in the Seattle 360 engine. It describes the blue note captured in a vocal sample, sliced into fragments, pitch-shifted, and reassembled inside an electronic production so that the emotional content of the blues bend persists even though the original vocal is unrecognizable. It is the first digitally mediated blue note: all forty-three prior deployments were analog (singers bending with voices, guitarists bending strings). The chopped bend passes through a digital intermediary. The emotional DNA of the blues tradition survives the technology. Chong the Nomad's vocal sample manipulation throughout Love Memo and Do We Make of This? produces chopped bends: R&B vocal inflection abstracted into pure texture that the listener feels without consciously hearing a traditional performance.

ancestors: Blue note -- the power bend (degree 43, Ann Wilson): maximum analog volume. The chopped bend is maximum digital density.
Blue note -- the sanctified bend (degree 42, Erica Campbell): spiritual upward vector. The chopped bend is dispersed into particles.
All forty-three prior blue note deployments (degrees 0-43)
genealogy.relationship: Forty-four deployments across forty-five degrees. The chopped bend follows the power bend (43) -- analog maximum followed by digital maximum. The chopped bend is the proof that the blue note tradition persists through any medium. Track deployment 45 at degree 45.

theory_nodes.concept: Electronic/R&B genre stage (Genre Stage 25 -- the first electronic-primary genre stage in the engine; electronic production as primary instrument for the first time in forty-five degrees; the R&B qualifier maps emotional substrate; the DAW replaces the voice, guitar, or horn as primary instrument; the engine enters the digital age)
examples: Chong the Nomad (degree 44) -- Electronic/R&B. Electronic primary (FL Studio productions, Roland SP-404A sampler, sawtooth synthesizers, 808 bass, chopped vocal samples, field recordings). R&B secondary (emotional content of love and loss, vocal intimacy buried in the mix, Love Memo as heartbreak diary).

Genre evolution: Rock/Soul (43) -> Electronic/R&B (44). The engine moves from the most analog genre (rock, physical voice projection) to the most digital genre (electronic, laptop production). The largest single-degree genre leap in the engine.

The R&B qualifier is essential: this is not techno or EDM. The emotional substrate comes from R&B's tradition of intimate vocal expression. The electronic delivery system carries the R&B emotional DNA into a digital framework.

Connection to the self-released model: Electronic/R&B is the first genre in the engine that does not require a studio, a label, or an institutional infrastructure. A laptop and an internet connection are sufficient.
theory_nodes.description: Electronic/R&B at degree 44 is Genre Stage 25 in the Seattle 360 engine -- the first time electronic production appears as a primary genre element. The engine has spent forty-four degrees in jazz, soul, funk, R&B, blues, neo-soul, gospel, and rock. At degree 44, the DAW (digital audio workstation) becomes the primary instrument. The R&B qualifier acknowledges the emotional content: Chong the Nomad's music carries soul vocal textures, R&B harmonic language, and love-and-loss thematic territory inside electronic production. This is not commercial EDM; it is intimate electronic music with R&B DNA. The genre jump from Rock/Soul (43) to Electronic/R&B (44) is the engine's largest single-degree genre leap -- from the most analog genre to the most digital.

ancestors: Rock/Soul (degree 43, Ann Wilson) -- Genre Stage 24. Analog rock. Electronic/R&B is the digital counterpart.
Pure R&B (degrees 32-33) -- R&B as primary without electronic qualifier. Electronic/R&B re-introduces R&B as secondary with electronic as primary.
Neo-Soul (degree 25, 40) -- electronic production elements within soul framework. Electronic/R&B inverts: soul/R&B elements within electronic framework.
genealogy.relationship: Twenty-five genre stages across forty-five degrees. Electronic/R&B is the first electronic-primary stage and the first genre requiring no institutional infrastructure (studio, label) to produce. The genre map now spans from jazz (degree 0) to electronic (degree 44). Track at degree 45.

theory_nodes.concept: Self-released economic model (first self-released primary artist in the engine -- no label for primary releases; Bandcamp economy; sync licensing as revenue; the dissolution of the gatekeeping apparatus that controlled every prior degree)
examples: Chong the Nomad (degree 44) -- Love Memo (2018) self-released digitally. Do We Make of This? (2024) self-released on Bandcamp at $11. Physical vinyl through Crane City Music (pressing operation, not A&R label). Hardly Art single is a one-off collaboration. 88rising/Interscope credit is producer-for-hire. No artist deal with any label for primary releases.
Revenue model: sync licensing (Apple, Nike, Singapore Airlines, Maybelline, TV shows), production for other artists (Wafia, Shang-Chi), DJ sets, live performance. Complete creative control retained.

The arc from Mercury Records (degree 0, Quincy Jones) to Bandcamp (degree 44, Chong the Nomad). The gatekeeping infrastructure dissolves across forty-five degrees. The engine documents the structural transformation of the music industry.
The self-released model is enabled by the same technology that enables the Electronic/R&B genre: the DAW and the internet. The tool that makes the music is the tool that distributes it.
theory_nodes.description: The self-released economic model at degree 44 marks the engine's first artist whose primary releases exist outside the label system entirely. Chong the Nomad self-releases on Bandcamp and streaming platforms, licensing to sync (Apple, Nike, Singapore Airlines) and producing for others (Wafia, Shang-Chi) for revenue while maintaining complete creative control. This represents the dissolution of the gatekeeping apparatus that controlled every prior degree: from Mercury Records (degree 0) to Bandcamp (degree 44), the engine documents the structural transformation of the music industry across forty-five degrees. The self-released model is enabled by the DAW and the internet -- the same technologies that enable the Electronic/R&B genre classification.

ancestors: BMG (degree 43, Ann Wilson) -- mid-major label. Self-released is the next step: no label at all.
Columbia/Sony streak (degrees 40-42) -- major label. Self-released breaks the label thread entirely.
Mushroom Records (degree 43, Heart debut) -- indie label. Self-released eliminates even indie label mediation.
genealogy.relationship: First self-released model in forty-five degrees. The engine's label thread reaches its logical terminus: the artist who doesn't need one. Track whether future degrees continue the self-released trend or return to label structures.

seattle_connection: THE BEDROOM ARCHITECT -- Chong the Nomad (degree 44) connects to Seattle as a bedroom producer who built an entire career from the city's cultural ecosystem without institutional gatekeeping. Raised in Renton/Kent (Seattle metro south suburbs). Studied music composition and gamelan at Cornish College of the Arts (same institution as Ann Wilson, degree 43, fifty years earlier). Worked as line cook at Kizuki Ramen on Capitol Hill. First shows at Capitol Hill Block Party, Sasquatch Music Festival, Upstream Festival (2018). Documented in NEWCOMER: A Seattle Hip-Hop Mixtape (Gary Campbell, 2020) -- from Belltown Yacht Club small crowd to CID Block Party audience of 500. Love Memo earned 2018 Top Album from Seattle Times, Stranger, KEXP, 107.7 The End. Split vinyl on Crane City Music with Stas THEE Boss. Visit Seattle 'I Know A Place' video host -- Capitol Hill businesses (Elliott Bay Book Company, Cafe Racer). KEXP pipeline: Midnight in a Perfect World DJ mix, Song of the Day, Street Sounds airplay. Sonic Guild grant 2020. Family operates Bumbu Truck (Indonesian street food). Pho Bac regular with family. CID Block Party performer. FIRST ELECTRONIC-PRIMARY GENRE at Genre Stage 25. FIRST SELF-RELEASED artist -- Bandcamp economy, no label. FIRST ASIAN-AMERICAN artist -- Indonesian-American heritage, Asian diaspora enters engine. FIRST NON-WESTERN MUSICAL TRADITION -- gamelan through Cornish study. TENTH WOMAN -- gender ratio 22.2%. Four women in five degrees (40, 42, 43, 44). E=5 reaches double digits (10 deployments). THE GRAIN as 45th acoustic element -- first digital-production-physics element. THE BEDROOM ARCHITECT as 45th artist-city pattern -- first technology-mediated city pattern. THE CHOPPED BEND as 44th blue note -- first digitally mediated blue note. Co-produced 'Lazy Susan' on Marvel Shang-Chi soundtrack (88rising/Interscope) featuring 21 Savage, Rich Brian, Warren Hue. Sync: Apple, Nike, Singapore Airlines, Maybelline. Opened for ODESZA, Death Cab for Cutie, The 1975, Japanese Breakfast. Production tools: FL Studio, Roland SP-404A, sawtooth synths, 808 bass, chopped vocal samples. Influences: Sophie Xeon ('my North star'), J Dilla, Daft Punk, gamelan. Energy distribution 2:13:7:12:10:2. Cornish-to-Cornish bridge (Wilson 43, Agustiano 44). Producer bookend (Jones degree 0, Agustiano degree 44).

theory_genealogy: The grain (NEW at degree 44): 45th acoustic element. First digital-production-physics element. Sound decomposed into grains, reassembled as shimmering texture. Track element 46 at degree 45.

The bedroom architect (NEW at degree 44): 45th artist-city pattern. First technology-mediated city pattern. Music made in private space, connected to city through technology. Track pattern 46 at degree 45.

Blue note -- the chopped bend (NEW at degree 44): 44th deployment. First digitally mediated blue note. Vocal sample sliced and pitch-shifted, blues bend persists through processing. Track deployment 45 at degree 45.
Electronic/R&B genre stage (NEW at degree 44): Genre Stage 25. First electronic-primary stage. DAW as primary instrument. R&B as emotional substrate. Track at degree 45.
Self-released model (NEW at degree 44): First self-released primary artist. Bandcamp economy. Label thread reaches terminus. Track at degree 45.
engine_sequencing: E=5 at 44: Distribution 2:13:7:12:10:2. 10th E=5 deployment -- double digits. Third-most frequent behind E=2 (13) and E=4 (12). Track at degree 45.
GENRE Stage 25 at degree 44: Electronic/R&B. First electronic-primary stage. Largest single-degree genre leap (Rock/Soul to Electronic/R&B). Track at degree 45.
FIRST ELECTRONIC-PRIMARY at degree 44: Genre expansion landmark. Electronic production stands as primary for first time in 45 degrees. Track electronic thread.
FIRST SELF-RELEASED at degree 44: Label thread terminus. Bandcamp economy. No A&R, no distribution deal, no label for primary releases. Track.
FIRST ASIAN-AMERICAN at degree 44: Indonesian-American heritage. Asian diaspora enters engine. 88rising/Shang-Chi connection. Track ethnic diversity.
FIRST NON-WESTERN TRADITION at degree 44: Gamelan enters the engine through Cornish study. Non-tempered tuning, cyclical rhythm, interlocking patterns. Track.
SEATTLE returns at degree 44: After Seattle at 43. Renton/Kent raised, Capitol Hill career. Cornish bridge to degree 43.
CORNISH COLLEGE bridge: Wilson (43) and Agustiano (44) both attended Cornish, ~50 years apart. Track institutional connections.
TENTH WOMAN at degree 44: Gender ratio 10:35 (~22.2%). Four women in five degrees (40, 42, 43, 44). Track.
MARVEL PRODUCTION CREDIT: Lazy Susan on Shang-Chi soundtrack. First MCU credit in engine. Track commercial reach.
All engine sequencing items from degrees 0-43
cultural_patterns: Artist-city taxonomy: FORTY-FIVE named patterns (adding The Bedroom Architect at degree 44). First technology-mediated pattern. Track.
Gender ratio: 10:35 (women:men). ~22.2%. Four women in five degrees (40, 42, 43, 44). Female. Track.
ELECTRONIC enters engine as primary genre: First electronic-primary stage (Genre Stage 25). Track electronic thread.
SELF-RELEASED enters engine: First artist without label for primary releases. Bandcamp economy. Track.
ASIAN-AMERICAN enters engine: Indonesian-American. First Southeast Asian heritage artist. Track ethnic diversity.
GAMELAN enters engine: First non-Western musical tradition. Studied at Cornish. Intentionally integrated into electronic production. Track.
CORNISH COLLEGE as institutional thread: Wilson (43) and Agustiano (44). Same school, two generations, two genres. Track.
PRODUCER BOOKEND: Jones (degree 0) and Agustiano (degree 44). Both producers. Mercury to Bandcamp. Track.
KEXP as tastemaking pipeline: Named Love Memo top album 2018. Midnight in a Perfect World mix. Song of the Day. Track.
QUEER IDENTITY documented: Agustiano identifies as queer woman of color (KEXP conversation). First explicitly queer-identified artist documented. Track.
SOPHIE XEON influence: 'My North star.' Radical sound design permission. Track influence genealogy.
BUMBU TRUCK and food truck culture: Immigrant family food business as biographical ground. Track.
All cultural pattern items from degrees 0-43
